FR: France is the most-visited country in the world!
DE: Germany has more than 1,500 different kinds of sausages.
IT: Italy is shaped like a boot and has over 1,500 lakes.
ES: Spain produces nearly half of the world's olive oil.
GB: The UK is made up of four countries: England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.
PT: Portugal is one of the oldest countries in Europe — over 900 years old!
NL: The Netherlands has more bicycles than people.
BE: Belgium has over 1,000 different kinds of chocolate.
CH: Switzerland has 7,000 lakes and is famous for cheese and watches.
AT: Austria's mountains are part of the Alps — perfect for skiing.
NO: Norway has the longest coastline of any country in Europe.
FI: Finland has over 180,000 lakes and is the home of saunas.
DK: Denmark is the birthplace of LEGO!
IS: Iceland is full of volcanoes, glaciers and waterfalls.
IE: Ireland is called the Emerald Isle because it is so green.
RU: Russia is the biggest country in the world by area.
UA: Ukraine has the largest sunflower fields in Europe.
TR: Turkey sits on two continents: Europe and Asia.
EG: Egypt is home to the famous pyramids of Giza.
MA: Morocco has the world's biggest desert at its doorstep — the Sahara.
ZA: South Africa has three capital cities.
KE: Kenya is famous for its safaris and the Great Migration.
NG: Nigeria is Africa's most populated country.
ET: Ethiopia uses its own calendar with 13 months!
GH: Ghana was the first African country to gain independence.
TZ: Tanzania has Mount Kilimanjaro, the tallest mountain in Africa.
MG: Madagascar has animals found nowhere else on Earth, like lemurs.
CN: China built the Great Wall, the longest wall in the world.
JP: Japan is made of more than 6,800 islands.
KR: South Korea has more than 70 percent of its land covered in mountains.
IN: India is the second most populated country in the world.
PK: Pakistan is home to K2, the second-tallest mountain on Earth.
BD: Bangladesh is home to the world's largest mangrove forest.
VN: Vietnam has more than 3,000 islands and gigantic cave systems.
TH: Thailand is the only Southeast Asian country never colonized by Europeans.
ID: Indonesia is made of more than 17,000 islands!
PH: The Philippines has over 7,000 islands too!
MY: Malaysia is one of only 17 'megadiverse' countries on Earth.
SG: Singapore is a city, a country, AND an island at the same time.
SA: Saudi Arabia is home to the holy cities of Mecca and Medina.
AE: The United Arab Emirates has the world's tallest building, the Burj Khalifa.
IL: Israel has the Dead Sea, the lowest place on Earth.
IR: Iran is home to one of the world's oldest civilizations — Persia.
IQ: Iraq is where one of the very first writing systems was invented.
AF: Afghanistan is famous for its beautiful blue lapis lazuli stones.
NP: Nepal is home to Mount Everest, the tallest mountain in the world.
MN: Mongolia has more horses than people.
KZ: Kazakhstan is the world's largest landlocked country.
AU: Australia is home to kangaroos, koalas, and over 10,000 beaches.
NZ: New Zealand has more sheep than people.
FJ: Fiji is made of 333 tropical islands.
PG: Papua New Guinea has more than 800 different languages.
US: The USA has 50 states and 5 territories.
CA: Canada has more lakes than the rest of the world combined!
MX: Mexico is the birthplace of chocolate.
BR: Brazil has the Amazon rainforest, home to millions of species.
AR: Argentina is named after silver — 'argentum' in Latin.
CL: Chile is the longest, thinnest country in the world.
PE: Peru is home to the ancient Inca city of Machu Picchu.
CO: Colombia is the only South American country with both Atlantic and Pacific coasts.
VE: Venezuela has Angel Falls, the world's tallest waterfall.
EC: Ecuador has the Galápagos Islands where Darwin studied evolution.
BO: Bolivia has the world's largest salt flat, Salar de Uyuni.
CR: Costa Rica has no army — they abolished it in 1948.
CU: Cuba is the largest island in the Caribbean.
JM: Jamaica is the birthplace of reggae music.
PA: Panama has a famous canal that joins two oceans.
BS: The Bahamas is made up of more than 700 islands.
DZ: Algeria is the largest country in Africa by area.
AO: Angola has rare giant sable antelopes.
BJ: Benin gave the world voodoo culture.
BW: Botswana has the largest population of elephants on Earth.
BF: Burkina Faso means 'land of honest people'.
BI: Burundi is one of the smallest countries in Africa.
CM: Cameroon is sometimes called 'Africa in miniature' — it has every climate.
CV: Cape Verde is a country of ten beautiful volcanic islands.
CF: Central African Republic is home to forest elephants and gorillas.
TD: Chad is mostly desert but also has the Sahel grasslands.
KM: Comoros is a country of just three small islands.
CG: Republic of the Congo straddles the equator.
CD: DR Congo has the second-largest rainforest on Earth.
CI: Côte d'Ivoire is the world's largest cocoa exporter.
DJ: Djibouti has lakes saltier than the ocean.
GQ: Equatorial Guinea is the only Spanish-speaking country in Africa.
ER: Eritrea's capital, Asmara, is a UNESCO World Heritage city.
SZ: Eswatini is one of the world's last absolute monarchies.
GA: Gabon's forests cover almost the whole country.
GM: The Gambia is Africa's smallest mainland country.
GN: Guinea is the source of West Africa's biggest rivers.
GW: Guinea-Bissau has beautiful islands called the Bijagos.
LS: Lesotho is a country completely surrounded by South Africa.
LR: Liberia was founded by freed American slaves in 1822.
LY: Libya is mostly desert — the Sahara.
MW: Malawi is called 'the warm heart of Africa'.
ML: Mali is home to Timbuktu, an ancient learning center.
MR: Mauritania has a train carrying iron ore that is over 2 km long!
MU: Mauritius is the only country where the dodo bird ever lived.
MZ: Mozambique has hundreds of kilometers of Indian Ocean coast.
NA: Namibia has the world's oldest desert, the Namib.
NE: Niger has the world's largest protected reserve, the Air and Ténéré.
RW: Rwanda is famous for its mountain gorillas.
ST: São Tomé and Príncipe is a country of cocoa-filled islands.
SN: Senegal's pink lake actually looks pink because of algae!
SC: Seychelles is a chain of 115 islands in the Indian Ocean.
SO: Somalia has the longest coastline of any African country on the mainland.
SS: South Sudan is the world's newest country (born in 2011).
SD: Sudan has more pyramids than Egypt!
TG: Togo grows lots of cocoa and coffee.
TN: Tunisia's old city of Tunis is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
UG: Uganda is home to half the world's mountain gorillas.
ZM: Zambia has Victoria Falls, one of the world's biggest waterfalls.
ZW: Zimbabwe is named after the Great Zimbabwe stone city ruins.
AL: Albania has more bunkers than any other country.
AD: Andorra is a tiny country tucked between France and Spain.
AM: Armenia was the first country to make Christianity its official religion.
AZ: Azerbaijan is called 'the land of fire' because of natural gas flames.
BY: Belarus is famous for its old castles and dense forests.
BA: Bosnia and Herzegovina has both a mainland and a tiny coast.
BG: Bulgaria is one of the oldest countries in Europe.
HR: Croatia has over 1,000 islands along its coast.
CY: Cyprus is one of the sunniest islands in Europe.
CZ: Czechia has more castles than almost any country in Europe.
EE: Estonia was one of the first countries to vote online.
GE: Georgia is one of the oldest wine-making countries in the world.
HU: Hungary has more hot springs than almost any other country.
XK: Kosovo is one of Europe's youngest countries.
LI: Liechtenstein is so small you can walk across it in a few hours.
LT: Lithuania has its very own scent — 'The Scent of Lithuania'.
LU: Luxembourg has the highest minimum wage in the European Union.
MT: Malta has temples older than the Egyptian pyramids.
MD: Moldova has the world's largest underground wine cellar.
MC: Monaco is the second-smallest country in the world.
ME: Montenegro is famous for dramatic mountains right next to the sea.
MK: North Macedonia is home to ancient Lake Ohrid.
RO: Romania has Transylvania, the legendary home of Dracula.
SM: San Marino is one of the world's oldest republics.
RS: Serbia is the birthplace of Nikola Tesla.
SK: Slovakia has the most castles per person in the world.
SI: Slovenia is more than half forest.
VA: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world.
BT: Bhutan measures Gross National Happiness instead of GDP.
BN: Brunei is one of the wealthiest countries thanks to oil.
KH: Cambodia is home to Angkor Wat, the world's largest religious building.
TL: Timor-Leste is one of the world's youngest countries (2002).
JO: Jordan has Petra, the ancient rose-red city carved into rock.
KW: Kuwait sits on huge oil reserves.
LA: Laos has more unexploded bombs than any country in the world.
LB: Lebanon's cedar tree is on its flag.
MV: Maldives is the lowest country on Earth — only 1.5 m above sea level.
MM: Myanmar's golden pagodas are scattered all over Bagan.
OM: Oman is one of the oldest independent states in the Arab world.
QA: Qatar hosted the 2022 FIFA World Cup.
LK: Sri Lanka was once called Ceylon — famous for tea.
SY: Syria's Damascus is one of the oldest inhabited cities in the world.
TM: Turkmenistan has a giant flaming crater called the 'Door to Hell'.
UZ: Uzbekistan has Samarkand, an ancient Silk Road city.
YE: Yemen has Socotra Island, full of plants found nowhere else.
PS: Palestine's Bethlehem and Jericho are some of the oldest cities on Earth.
KP: North Korea uses its own calendar that started in 1912.
FM: Micronesia is a country of over 600 tiny islands.
KI: Kiribati is the first country to see the sun each day.
MH: Marshall Islands sit halfway between Hawaii and Australia.
NR: Nauru is the world's smallest island country.
PW: Palau has lakes filled with stingless jellyfish.
WS: Samoa was the first country to see the new day after moving the dateline.
SB: Solomon Islands has hundreds of unspoiled tropical islands.
TO: Tonga is the only Pacific country that was never colonized.
TV: Tuvalu makes lots of money by renting out its '.tv' domain name.
VU: Vanuatu has live volcanoes you can hike up to.
AG: Antigua and Barbuda has 365 beaches — one for every day of the year.
BB: Barbados is the birthplace of rum.
BZ: Belize has the second-largest barrier reef in the world.
DM: Dominica has more rivers than any other Caribbean island.
DO: Dominican Republic shares an island with Haiti.
SV: El Salvador is the smallest country in mainland Americas.
GD: Grenada is called 'the Isle of Spice' — nutmeg is on the flag!
GT: Guatemala has 33 volcanoes — some still active.
GY: Guyana has Kaieteur Falls, one of the most powerful waterfalls.
HT: Haiti was the first country to abolish slavery (in 1804).
HN: Honduras has stunning Mayan ruins at Copán.
KN: Saint Kitts and Nevis is the smallest country in the Americas.
LC: Saint Lucia has twin volcanic peaks called the Pitons.
VC: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines is a chain of 32 islands.
NI: Nicaragua is home to the largest lake in Central America.
PY: Paraguay has Itaipú, one of the world's biggest hydroelectric dams.
SR: Suriname is the smallest country in South America.
TT: Trinidad and Tobago is the birthplace of the steelpan drum.
UY: Uruguay was the first country to win the FIFA World Cup (1930).
BH: Bahrain is a tiny island country in the Persian Gulf.
